title: Mailwoman eval scores ledger
description: Versioned per-(model, eval-set) evaluation scores for the mailwoman neural parser. One file lives at evals/scores-by-version.json; one `runs[]` entry per training run that produced shippable weights or a measured baseline.

schema_version.description: Bumps only when a breaking change to this schema requires migration.

run_id.description: Stable opaque identifier for the run, e.g. 'stage1-coarse-2026-05-18-rocm62'. Unique within the file.

model_version.description: Semver of the shipped @mailwoman/neural-weights-* package this run corresponds to. Pre-release suffixes allowed for non-shipped baselines.

model_path.description: Canonical npm package path or relative filesystem path, e.g. '@mailwoman/neural-weights-en-us@0.1.0' or 'packages/neural-weights-en-us'.

corpus_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the corpus MANIFEST.json that produced this run.

eval_set_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the eval-set tar/manifest. Prevents silent eval-set drift.

trained_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp the training run completed.

hardware.description: Free-text hardware + framework summary, e.g. 'AMD Radeon 780M, ROCm 6.2, torch 2.5.1+rocm6.2'.

notes.description: Free-text annotation: notable changes vs prior run, training quirks, calibration notes, etc.

exact_match.description: Fraction of eval rows where every component matches the gold value exactly (case-insensitive, trimmed).

per_component.description: Per-component (country, region, locality, ...) metrics. Keys are the component names; values are the metric block.

calibration_buckets.description: Calibration buckets sorted by descending confidence. Mandatory for shipped releases; optional for ad-hoc baselines.

adversarial_set.description: Per-named-adversarial-case scores (e.g. 'buffalo_buffalo', 'saint_petersburg'). Tracks the bitter-lesson kryptonite cases distinctly. Keys are case-name slugs.

support.description: Number of eval rows where this component had a non-empty gold value.

observed_accuracy.description: Observed accuracy of predictions whose confidence fell in [min_conf, max_conf]. A well-calibrated bucket has observed_accuracy ≈ (min_conf + max_conf) / 2.

Unit.description: A scalar in the unit interval [0, 1].
